Dimension research SETTLED (config-verified). Qwen-Edit-2511 input dims must be ÷32: math floor=16 (VAE 8x spatial dim_mult[1,2,4,4] x transformer patch_size 2) but diffusers calculate_dimensions() enforces 32 (issue #12997). Non-÷32 = ValueError (no crop/pad). ImageScaleToTotalPixels (class_type ImageScaleToTotalPixels) MOVED to nodes_post_processing.py on master + gained resolution_steps int (def 1, advanced): width=round(W*scale_by/steps)*steps snaps to multiple. OLD node (nodes_images.py, no longer there / older installs) = bare round, NO snap. resolution_steps = dimension snap granularity NOT sampling steps. RECOMMEND: megapixels=1.0 + resolution_steps=32; VERIFY canvas node has resolution_steps else update ComfyUI. Verified vs raw ComfyUI GitHub master (one sub-agent's stale checkout wrongly said custom-pack-only). Dump: research/dimensions.md, settled: docs/models/qwen-edit/dimensions.md.

CORRECTION (deeper pipeline-source pass): non-÷16 is a WARNING + silent crop/pad, NOT a hard ValueError (I over-stated). calculate_dimensions rounds ITS bucket dims to ÷32 (conservative, not a validation gate). ComfyUI hard-errors separately on attention-reshape shape mismatch (#9421). Practical guidance unchanged: feed ÷32 (resolution_steps=32) to dodge crop/pad + bucket drift. Verified archdata: VAE AutoencoderKLQwenImage f8 (2^len temperal_downsample=2^3), z_dim16; transformer patch_size2, in_channels64(=16*4), 60 blocks, joint_attention_dim3584. Added ComfyUI landmines to docs: black-image=latent_mean/std not applied (#11865), batched VAE-encode 1-img bug fixed ca68660, inpaint VAE unsupported (#9605).

USER LIVE TEST (standalone ComfyUI, G:/CubricModels weights): full-precision TE qwen_2.5_vl_7b pairs BADLY with fp8 transformer — quality poor. Hypothesis: TE precision should track transformer precision; full/bf16 TE reserved for bf16 High tier, fp8_scaled TE for fp8/int8 tiers. Downloads: fp8mixed(20,533,762,817)+int8_convrot(20,499,083,824)+full TE(16,584,415,576) landed/verified in G:. bf16(40,861,031,560) still to grab via browser (browser ~42MB/s vs curl-throttled 1.7MB/s — big weights = browser). User now A/B-ing int8 vs fp8 transformer. Tier mapping still open pending these tests. LESSON: check user C:/standalone dirs BEFORE downloading (wasted an fp8mixed dl — already on C:).

USER A/B fp8mixed vs int8_convrot (6-cell grid, TE constant). SPEED int8 wins: 4step fp8 61s/int8 64s (~tie), 8step fp8 108s/int8 103s, raw fp8 486s/int8 386s (int8 ~100s / ~20% faster). QUALITY int8 = equal-or-better all 3 step counts (int8 4step sharper faces/composition than soft fp8 4step; raw near-identical). VERDICT: INT8 (qwen_image_edit_2511_int8_convrot) is the transformer. bf16 High-tier test still pending (int8 raw 386s = current no-accel candidate; bf16 may beat quality). Tier LoRA: 4step=Low, 8step=Balanced, raw/no-accel=High.

fp8mixed DELETED (lost A/B to int8). bf16 landed+verified via browser (40,861,031,560 B). G:/CubricModels/diffusion_models now holds exactly 2 survivors: int8_convrot (20,499,083,824 = Low/Balanced) + bf16 (40,861,031,560 = High candidate). LAST test pending: int8-raw (386s) vs bf16-raw for High tier.

TE-precision axis: full TE qwen_2.5_vl_7b FAILS with int8 too (hallucinates + stretches) — same as fp8. So fp8_scaled TE (9.38GB) is the correct pairing for BOTH quantized transformers (fp8, int8). Full TE (16.58GB) now on trial ONLY vs bf16 — if it fails there too, DELETE full TE. User starting bf16 4-step, testing both TEs.

bf16 4-step TE A/B: TE Full 86s / TE fp8 68s (fp8 18s faster AND better anatomy — Full stretches left subject's torso, same failure as fp8/int8). FULL TE LOSES ON ALL 3 TRANSFORMERS (fp8, int8, bf16) — hallucinate+stretch every time. DECISION: kill full TE qwen_2.5_vl_7b (16.58GB). Model ships ONE text encoder for all tiers: qwen_2.5_vl_7b_fp8_scaled (9.38GB, already on G:). Simplifies deps.

int8 vs bf16(TEfp8) grid, Lightning LoRA: 4step int8 64s/bf16 68s (~tie quality+speed), 8step int8 103s/bf16 141s (int8 38s faster, quality comparable), raw int8 386s(20step)/bf16 PENDING. So far bf16 slower every step-count for no visible 4/8-step quality gain. bf16 justified ONLY if bf16-raw clearly beats int8-raw. NOTE: raw tested at 20 steps, base is ~40-step model — 20 may undercook both; if testing 40, run BOTH transformers for fair compare. bf16's edge (if any) likely only at native ~40 steps.

PLAN: after bf16-raw-20step, user runs 40-step raw for BOTH int8 + bf16 (native step count) = decisive High-tier factor. If bf16 doesn't clearly beat int8 at 40 steps, bf16 trashed and High tier also = int8 (whole model on ONE transformer int8_convrot + ONE TE fp8_scaled).

20step raw times in: int8 386s / bf16 559s (bf16 +173s / +45% slower). Side-by-side raw zoom (int8 L vs bf16 R): faces near-identical, anatomy clean both (stretch gone = was full-TE, now deleted), differences COSMETIC only (bf16 slightly smoother skin, int8 slightly more micro-detail). bf16 paying big time-cost for no tier-level quality win. 40-step raw both = final decider (slow — bf16 ~11min+). Trend: if 40step also cosmetic-only, int8 takes High too + bf16 trashed.

DECISIVE: bf16 40step raw 1045s vs 20step 559s — 40step did NOT improve, slightly WORSE (left subject lips over-cooked toward teeth = same over-process artifact as full-TE). Base Qwen-Edit-2511 wants the LIGHTNING LORA path, not high raw steps; raw-high drifts. bf16 gave zero tier-level quality win at any step (4/8/20/40) for +45-87% time +18GB VRAM. VERDICT: bf16 TRASHED. Model ships int8_convrot + fp8_scaled TE, ONE transformer ALL tiers. High tier = int8 (raw-20 or 8-step LoRA), NOT bf16, NOT 40-step. 40step-int8 test SKIPPED as redundant (raw-high proven worse than LoRA). User's earlier bf16=High hunch inverted by data.

VERIFIED FROM ComfyUI /history (not assumed): Input_Image + Input_Image_2 inject real filesystem paths, Input_Image_3 self-gates when empty; chip order maps to encoder image1/image2; Input_Tier drives BOTH the model-path switch and the step-count switch (20/8/4) off one MpiInt; the 4-step/8-step Lightning LoRAs sit in the correct slots (the earlier inversion is fixed). USER CONFIRMED all style LoRAs render correctly, INCLUDING the Anime 2D/3D pair whose LoRA filenames do not match their labels (slot2 Qwen-Anime-V2 = 'Anime 3D', slot3 animal_style = 'Anime 2D') - do NOT 'correct' that mapping to match filenames. MODEL BEHAVIOUR (reproduced in app AND native ComfyUI, so a model limit not a wiring bug): strong at COMBINING images, weak at single-image instruction edits - small attribute edits land, larger rewrites get ignored or degrade framing/faces; images must be referenced BY NUMBER (opposite of Krea2). Description + docs/models/qwen-edit/README.md updated to say so. Commits: ed79fb2f, fac21493, 2ef5e816.
